_meta.purpose: Answers Pranav's default responses to standard job-application form fields. Copy this file to ~/.job_search/answers.json and fill any TODO fields. Never commit the real file — it lives outside the repo.
conventions: Each field has a picked default in the string value.
Alternative options are listed alongside as _<field>_options arrays.
TODO markers mean the user must supply it before /apply can run cleanly.
Fields under 'sensitive' MUST NEVER be auto-filled by /apply — always leave blank.

location.street_address_1: TODO — sometimes required by Workday/iCIMS; leave blank otherwise

work_authorization._sponsorship_now_or_future_answer: Yes — I will require H-1B sponsorship in the future. My F-1 STEM OPT is valid through July 2028.

education.gpa: TODO — 3.x/4.0 or leave 'Prefer not to disclose'

diversity_eeo._note: Every field here is legally optional. All defaults are 'Prefer not to say' unless you want to disclose.
_disability_options: No, I do not have a disability

typical_short_answers._note: Fallback stock answers for questions where the JD has no specific hook. The /apply command should read the JD first and write something more tailored using the voice rules — these are used only when the question is completely generic (e.g. 'Anything else?').
typical_short_answers.greatest_strength: I own vertical slices end to end — from design through production and iteration on real user data. I get comfortable with the whole stack rather than one layer.
typical_short_answers.greatest_weakness: I over-invest in eval and observability early. It slows the first few days of a project but pays off later, and I've gotten better at picking when it's worth it.

sensitive._note: NEVER auto-fill any of these. If a form asks, /apply should skip the field and log it for manual review.
